technique: delay smoking by 10 minutes, drink a glass of water, use nicotine gum

symptom: increased craving under pressure or anxiety
technique: deep breathing (4-7-8 technique), brief walk, call a support person
chew sugar-free gum, brush teeth immediately, change post-meal routine

symptom: peer pressure or seeing others smoke
technique: rehearse refusal script, hold a non-alcoholic drink, step away briefly
keep hands busy (doodle, stress ball), short physical activity
avoid or reduce alcohol during quit attempt, have a plan before social events

symptom: smoking during work breaks or deadlines
technique: replace smoke break with 5-minute walk, progressive muscle relaxation

symptom: smoking to cope with sadness or frustration
technique: identify emotion before smoking, journaling, contact therapist or helpline

symptom: cigarette feels necessary with morning coffee
technique: switch to tea temporarily, change location of morning drink
schedule a phone call or activity during high-risk hours (4-6 PM)

symptom: guilt and resuming full smoking after one cigarette
technique: normalize slip, use it as data not failure, restart quit plan immediately
urge surfing — observe craving without acting, cravings peak at 3-5 min then fade
